LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MAY-23-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO ALL SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (SAC) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ARTURO CORTEZ IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR ALLEGED FEHA AND LABOR CODE VIOLATIONS AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO, CSR# 5229 (TEL: 415-828-1919 / EMAIL: PEGGYTSUJIMOTO@GMAIL.COM) =(301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. PASHA VAFAEI, Esq. of MVP Trial Lawyers (tel: 213-770-7700) for Plaintiff.
Atty. GABRIELA GONZALEZ, Esq. of Narayan Travelstead Ku P.C. (tel: 650-403-0150) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Counsel Pasha Vafaei presents oral motion for 170.1 challenge. The Court denies the oral motion for 170.1 challenge. Counsel Pasha Vafaei presents oral motion for 170.6 challenge. Oath is administered to Counsel Pasha Vafaei. Counsel Pasha Vafaei testifies. The Court denies oral motion for 170.6 challenge, as it was not properly invoked.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

Defendant The Regents of the University of California's demurrer to all seven causes of action in the second amended complaint (SAC) filed by plaintiff Arturo Cortez is overruled as to the first through fifth causes of action for alleged FEHA and Labor Code violations and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth and seventh causes of action for intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress.

The Regents' argument that Texas law applies to Mr. Cortez's claims lacks merit because the SAC alleges that all of Mr. Cortez's work was performed, albeit much of it remotely, for a work site in California and that allegation must be accepted as true on a demurrer.

The Regents' argument that the Labor Code claim is barred by Mr. Cortez's failure to exhaust administrative remedies lacks merit because there is no allegation in the SAC regarding administrative remedies and there has been no request for judicial notice regarding any such remedies. On a demurrer a court may only consider the allegations of the operative complaint and matters that are judicially noticeable.

Mr. Cortez has adequately alleged each of his FEHA claims by alleging sufficient ultimate facts showing that he was discriminated against based on his gender, sexually harassed by a supervisor, retaliated against due to his protected activity, and the Regents failed to prevent that discrimination, sexual harassment, and retaliation.

Mr. Cortez's emotional distress claims are barred by the exclusivity provision of the Workers' Compensation Act. (Shoemaker v. Myers (1990) 52 Cal. 2d 1, 25). Moreover, Mr. Cortez's opposition memorandum stated that he intended to dismiss his negligent infliction claim.

The Court grants leave to amend for plaintiff to allege wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Plaintiff has 20 day to file a third amended complaint from notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR# 5229 (tel: 415-828-1919 / email: peggytsujimoto@gmail.com) =(301/HEK)
